questions.question_text: A landlord in Rhode Island refuses to rent to a tenant because they plan to pay with a Section 8 voucher. Is this lawful?
questions.correct_answer: No, lawful source of income is protected
distractors: Yes, landlords may choose payment methods
Only if the unit has fewer than 4 units
Only if the voucher amount is below market rent
questions.explanation: Rhode Island's Fair Housing Practices Act (RIGL §34-37-1) protects tenants from discrimination based on lawful source of income, which includes Section 8 housing vouchers. Landlords cannot refuse tenancy solely because a tenant uses government assistance to pay rent.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a protected class under Rhode Island law but NOT under federal fair housing law?
distractors: Race
National origin
Familial status
questions.explanation: Rhode Island's Fair Housing Practices Act extends protection beyond federal classes to include gender identity/expression, sexual orientation, marital status, lawful source of income, military status, country of ancestral origin, housing status, age, and domestic abuse victim status.

questions.question_text: A property manager asks a prospective tenant about their citizenship status during a rental application in 2026. What is the legal consequence?
questions.correct_answer: It violates RI law prohibiting such inquiries
distractors: It is permitted if done uniformly to all applicants
It is allowed if the property has more than 4 units
It is required for federally assisted housing
questions.explanation: Effective July 2025, Rhode Island prohibits landlords and agents from inquiring about a prospective tenant's immigration or citizenship status. This applies regardless of the property size or how uniformly the question is asked.

questions.question_text: An agent advertises a rental listing as ideal for young professionals. A retired veteran with a housing voucher applies and is rejected. How many RI protected classes could this scenario implicate?
questions.correct_answer: At least three: age, military, source of income
distractors: Only one: lawful source of income protection
Only two: age and military status together
None, advertising preferences are not discriminatory
questions.explanation: This scenario could implicate age (targeting young people), military status (veteran), and lawful source of income (housing voucher). All three are protected classes under Rhode Island's Fair Housing Practices Act, and the advertising language could serve as evidence of discriminatory intent.

questions.question_text: Under the RI Fair Housing Practices Act, the right to equal housing is classified as which type of right?
distractors: A contractual right
A statutory privilege
A property right
questions.explanation: Rhode Island classifies the right to equal housing as a civil right under RIGL §34-37-1 et seq. This designation provides strong legal protections and remedies for individuals who experience housing discrimination.

questions.question_text: A buyer believes they were denied a mortgage because of their marital status. Which Rhode Island agency should they file a complaint with?
distractors: Department of Business Regulation
RI Housing and Mortgage Finance Corp
Office of the Attorney General
questions.explanation: The Rhode Island Commission for Human Rights (RICHR) enforces the state's fair housing laws, which cover the sale, rental, and financing of housing. RICHR investigates complaints and can impose penalties for violations.

questions.question_text: After filing a complaint with RICHR, a tenant learns the investigation found probable cause. What additional legal remedy is available to the tenant?
distractors: Only RICHR administrative remedies
An appeal to the Governor's office
Mandatory arbitration through DBR
questions.explanation: In addition to the RICHR administrative process, Rhode Island law allows individuals to file private lawsuits in court for fair housing violations. This dual-track approach gives complainants multiple avenues for seeking justice.

questions.question_text: A real estate agent steers minority buyers away from certain neighborhoods. Which transactions does the RI Fair Housing Practices Act cover in this scenario?
questions.correct_answer: Sale, rental, and financing of housing
distractors: Only the sale of residential property
Only rental transactions over $500/month
Only transactions involving licensed agents
questions.explanation: The RI Fair Housing Practices Act applies broadly to the sale, rental, and financing of housing. Steering buyers based on protected characteristics violates the Act regardless of the transaction type or property value.

questions.question_text: A landlord with a domestic abuse victim as a tenant tells the victim they must leave because of police calls to the property. Under RI law, what is this landlord's legal exposure?
distractors: No exposure if lease has a nuisance clause
Liability only if tenant has a restraining order
No liability since police calls affect neighbors
questions.explanation: Domestic abuse victim status is a protected class under Rhode Island's Fair Housing Practices Act. A landlord cannot evict or penalize a tenant solely because they are a victim of domestic abuse, even if police are called to the property as a result of abuse incidents.

questions.question_text: RICHR completes an investigation and finds a property management company systematically excluded applicants with housing vouchers. What can RICHR do?
distractors: Only issue a warning letter
Refer the case to federal HUD exclusively
Revoke the company's business license
questions.explanation: RICHR has the authority to investigate complaints and impose penalties for fair housing violations. Systematic exclusion of applicants based on lawful source of income violates the RI Fair Housing Practices Act, and RICHR can take enforcement action directly.
